clinical_notes.summary: 28yo healthy female presenting for evaluation of recurrent palpitations over past 2 months. Episodes of rapid heartbeat lasting 5-30 seconds, 2-3 times per week, triggered by caffeine or stress. Occasional lightheadedness during episodes, no syncope. No cardiac history. Consumes 3-4 cups coffee daily. Exercises regularly without limitation. No allergies, no regular medications. HR 78 bpm, BP 110/68 mmHg, SpO2 99%, BMI 22.4. Cardiac and pulmonary exam normal.

chest_xray.summary: No acute cardiopulmonary abnormality. Lung fields clear bilaterally. Heart size normal, cardiothoracic ratio 0.44. No mediastinal widening. No pleural effusions or pneumothorax. Entirely normal chest radiograph.

ecg.summary: Normal sinus rhythm at 78 bpm. Normal axis. PR interval 148ms. QRS duration 82ms. Isolated premature atrial complexes (PACs) observed — 2 per tracing with normal P-wave morphology. No ST-segment changes. QTc 398ms. No delta waves or pre-excitation pattern. Findings consistent with benign PACs.

echo.summary: Normal left ventricular size and function, LVEF 64%. No structural abnormalities. Normal diastolic function. No significant valvular disease. Right heart normal. No pericardial effusion. Left atrial size normal at 3.3cm. No evidence of cardiomyopathy or accessory pathway substrate.

heart_sounds.summary: S1 and S2 normal. Regular rate and rhythm at 78 bpm. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs. Occasional early beat consistent with PACs noted during auscultation. No S3 or S4. Otherwise entirely normal cardiac examination.

lab_results.summary: CBC within normal limits. Hemoglobin 13.2 g/dL. BMP normal. TSH 1.6 mIU/L (normal, ruling out thyroid dysfunction). Fasting glucose 86 mg/dL (normal). Magnesium 2.0 mEq/L (normal). Ferritin 28 ng/mL (low normal). Urine hCG negative. No electrolyte abnormalities identified.

medication.summary: No regular prescription medications. Daily multivitamin. No drug allergies. No medications initiated at this visit. Counseled on caffeine reduction to ≤1 cup coffee daily and stress management techniques. Reassurance provided regarding benign nature of PACs. No antiarrhythmic therapy indicated.
